_meta.authored_from: §3.6 (kid header, multi-key verifier pattern) + §3.3 (alg: dir, enc: A256GCM, HKDF derivation), cross-service-contract 2026-05-25
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.revised: 2026-06-10 — claim shape locked to the shipped emerald-contracts DeviceClaims serialization (flat device_id/device_platform, not the pre-crate nested device:{id,platform}); sampleTokens regenerated via the N-API binding; missing-kid expectation updated to the crate's header-parse hard-reject.
notes: Tests the §3.6 verifier dispatch pattern: decode_protected_header(token).kid → keyMap[kid] → decrypt.
Key derivation: HKDF-Extract+Expand (RFC 5869, SHA-256) over the raw secret with salt='' and info='eex/device-token/v<N>'.
v1 test secret (32 bytes UTF-8): DEVICE_TEST_SECRET_V1_32CHARS_XX
v2 test secret (32 bytes UTF-8): DEVICE_TEST_SECRET_V2_32CHARS_XX
alg: dir means the encrypted key component (part[1] of the compact serialization) is empty.
JWE tokens are NOT deterministic (random 12-byte IV per encrypt). Consumers MUST decrypt and compare the claims object, not the token bytes.

expectedClaims keys use the canonical wire serialization of emerald-contracts::device_token::DeviceClaims (snake_case, flat device_id/device_platform). The N-API binding exposes the same fields camelCased (deviceId/devicePlatform); the wire bytes inside the JWE are snake_case.
ULID charset check: 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGS, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGT, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGV, 01HWXYZ01234567890ABCDEFGW — all Crockford Base32, no I/L/O/U.

Executed by: crates/emerald-contracts/tests/vectors.rs (Rust) and server/services/contractsBinding.test.ts (TS via the production N-API binding — the same functions server/session.ts mint/verify call).

vectors.name: device-v1-known-kid-passes
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v1' and the correct v1 key decrypts successfully. Tests the happy path of the §3.6 kid-dispatch verifier: decode the protected header, look up kid='device-v1' in the key map, decrypt with the derived v1 key.
vectors.decryptionNote: Decrypt with keyMap['device-v1'].derivedKeyHex. Assert decrypted claims match expectedClaims. Token bytes are NOT stable (random IV); do not compare token bytes.
vectors.sampleTokenNote: Pre-generated with the N-API binding (deviceTokenEncrypt) using derivedKeyHex for device-v1. Test harnesses SHOULD generate a fresh token from expectedClaims + kid + derivedKeyHex and verify round-trip; the sampleToken is a cross-language sanity fixture that MUST also decrypt to expectedClaims.

vectors.name: device-v2-known-kid-passes
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v2' and the correct v2 key decrypts successfully. Tests that the verifier correctly dispatches to the v2 key during a rotation overlap window. Both v1 and v2 tokens must be accepted simultaneously.
vectors.decryptionNote: Decrypt with keyMap['device-v2'].derivedKeyHex. Assert decrypted claims match expectedClaims.
vectors.sampleTokenNote: Pre-generated with the N-API binding (deviceTokenEncrypt) using derivedKeyHex for device-v2. Same note as device-v1-known-kid-passes.

vectors.name: unknown-kid-hard-rejects
vectors.description: Token with kid='device-v99' (not in keyMap) must be hard-rejected before decryption is attempted. Tests the §3.6 guard: if the kid lookup misses, fail immediately with a clear error. Do NOT attempt to iterate all keys — that enables kid enumeration attacks.
vectors.errorNote: The verifier MUST throw/return an error containing 'unknown kid' or equivalent (Rust: DeviceTokenError::UnknownKid) before any decryption attempt. The token bytes need not be a valid JWE — the kid check fires first on the decoded protected header.
vectors.syntheticTokenNote: This is a structurally plausible JWE compact with kid='device-v99' in the protected header. The ciphertext/tag are dummy bytes — decryption would fail even if attempted. The test asserts the verifier rejects on kid lookup, not on decryption.

vectors.name: missing-kid-hard-rejects
vectors.description: Token with no kid field in the protected header must be hard-rejected. The canonical crate requires kid at header-parse time: decode_protected_header fails (Rust: JweError::BadHeader) before any key lookup or decryption. This is stricter than the legacy §3.6 reference (coalesce absent kid to '' then miss the map) — both reject, the crate rejects earlier.
vectors.errorNote: Reject fires at protected-header parse — a token without kid cannot be dispatched to any key. Harnesses assert a hard failure before decryption; the exact error label is binding-specific (Rust Jwe(BadHeader), N-API throws).
vectors.syntheticTokenNote: Protected header has alg+enc but no kid field. Header parse fails closed — the verifier must reject.
